name: ASAP2s
description: Higher-sensitivity ASAP1 variant that trades speed for larger fluorescence responses.

brightnessData.note: Relative molecular brightness under 2P excitation at 920 nm in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (JEDI-2P paper Fig. 2I, primary subject JEDI-2P). Bar means: ASAP2s ≈ 1.00 (normalization reference), ASAP3 ≈ 0.90, JEDI-2P ≈ 1.53 → ASAP2s/ASAP3 = 1.00/0.90 ≈ 1.11 (ASAP2s brighter than ASAP3; the ASAP2s-vs-ASAP3 difference is n.s., n = 6). Direction verified by rendering Fig. 2I.
ASAP2s matched the brightness of ASAP1 under both one- and two-photon illumination in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (Fig. 1—figure supplement 2A,B).

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean of the two-exponential fit (HEK293A, 22°C). Depolarization (−70→+30 mV): τfast 5.2 ms (56%), τslow 63 ms (44%) → 30.6 ms. Repolarization (+30→−70 mV): τfast 24 ms (49%), τslow 106 ms (51%) → 65.8 ms. Previously stored values (5.2/24) were the fast components only.

Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 32-35°C (Villette 2019 Table 1, ASAP2s column). Activation: τ_fast=1.50 ms (67%), τ_slow=7.80 ms → weighted on=3.58 ms. Deactivation: τ_fast=3.40 ms (63%), τ_slow=13.10 ms → weighted off=6.99 ms. Previously stored the fast components only (1.5/3.4).

Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 32-35°C in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (JEDI-2P paper, Table S2; ASAP2s column). Activation: τ_fast=3.78 ms (93.6%), τ_slow=132 ms → weighted on=11.99 ms. Deactivation: τ_fast=7.59 ms (91.7%), τ_slow=170 ms → weighted off=21.07 ms. Previously stored the fast components only (3.78/7.59).

Amplitude-weighted mean of the bi-exponential fit at 21-23°C in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (JEDI-2P paper, Table S2; ASAP2s column). Activation: τ_fast=6.39 ms (91.3%), τ_slow=125 ms → weighted on=16.71 ms. Deactivation: τ_fast=12.7 ms (87.1%), τ_slow=143 ms → weighted off=29.51 ms. Previously stored the fast components only (6.39/12.7).

dynamicRangeData.note: Steady-state ΔF/F for a 1 s, 100 mV depolarization from −70 to +30 mV in HEK293A cells (one-photon, 22°C).

sensitivityData.note: −12.2 ± 0.5% per single AP in cultured hippocampal neurons (one-photon).
−15.0 ± 0.6% per evoked AP under two-photon (FEVIR) imaging in cultured neurons.

subthresholdData.note: Subthreshold voltage sensitivity ≈ 0.15 %/mV, from the Fig. 5G peak-response-vs-depolarization plot in organotypic hippocampal slices under 2P (462 Hz). ASAP2s ΔF/F peaks were ~0.9%/1.35%/2.2%/3.15% for 5/10/15/20 mV subthreshold depolarization waveforms (least-squares slope 0.154 %/mV, read from the bar graph). This is a 2P slice measurement of responses to subthreshold-waveform commands (not a 1P near-rest voltage-clamp ramp), so it may not be directly comparable to 1P subthreshold slopes.

photostabilityData.note: Fluorescence remaining after 1 min of continuous widefield 470–490 nm illumination at 11 mW/mm² in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells (F/F0 ≈ 0.71 at t=60 s of the Fig. 1—figure supplement 3A bleach curve; ASAP2s green trace, biexponential τ1=121 s / τ2=1017 s). Consistent with the digitized photobleach curve stored for the same figure.

apWidthData.sample: Organotypic hippocampal slice cultures, current-triggered single AP, two-photon RAMP (FEVIR)
apWidthData.note: Optical single-AP duration (full-width at half-maximum) stated in the origin paper text: 'ASAP2s kinetics were slower, with a time-to-peak of 6.0 ms and a duration of 21.1 ms' (vs 3.8 ms for ASAP1), measured under two-photon FEVIR (RAMP microscopy) in organotypic hippocampal slice cultures, oversampled at 3700 Hz, at room temperature (22°C) (Fig. 6D,E). Much broader than the 6.8 ms HEK293A command-waveform value (JEDI-1P paper) because it reflects a real neuronal AP convolved with ASAP2s's slow repolarization kinetics under two-photon imaging.

apWidthData.sample: Rat, cultured hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Optical spike width (FWHM of the optical AP) read from the Fig. 2J bar graph in cultured hippocampal neurons responding to current-triggered APs, 1P widefield. ASAP1 ≈ 9.0 ms, ASAP2s ≈ 17.4 ms; the electrical AP widths in the same neurons were 4.2 ms (ASAP1) / 4.6 ms (ASAP2s) per the caption, so the optical widths are broadened by the sensors' response kinetics (ASAP2s' slower repolarization gives the wider optical spike). Imaging rate is the Methods-stated 1 kHz widefield optical-AP rate; the width is kinetics-dominated (>> the 1 ms frame period), not frame-rate-limited. Recording temperature not stated for this panel. Distinct from the 2P/organotypic-slice value (Fig. 6E) and the HEK293 value.

apWidthData.sample: Rat, cultured hippocampal neurons, current-evoked single AP, room temperature, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Optical spike width at half-maximum, read from the Fig. 2F bar graph (0–20 ms axis; ASAP2s ≈ 17.3 ms, ASAP3 ≈ 12.7 ms). Cross-measured in the ASAP3 origin paper (Villette 2019 Cell, primary subject ASAP3); ASAP2s (n = 4) and ASAP3 (n = 5) responses to current-evoked APs in cultured hippocampal neurons (electrical AP FWHM 5.0 ± 0.1 ms for ASAP2s, per Fig. 2E caption). Widefield 1P imaging. The width is kinetics-dominated; the paper reports only a 1–2 kHz imaging range so samplingRate is omitted. Independent replicate of the ~17.4 ms eLife Fig. 2J value from a different lab/paper.

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, cultured hippocampal neurons, current-triggered single AP, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Half-width of the optically recorded action potential = 12.3 ± 0.7 ms (n = 6), text-stated in Milosevic 2020 eNeuro (which labels the sensor 'ASAP2' but images AAV CamK2-ASAP2s / hSyn-ASAP2s per the Fig. 1B legend, so it is ASAP2s). Same-paper values: Archon1 6.1 ms, ASAP1 8.5 ms, Bongwoori-Pos6 33.1 ms. Optical AP recorded at 500 frames/s (0.5 kHz) widefield; the optical AP half-width is ~8× wider than the simultaneous whole-cell electrical AP half-width (766 ± 121% distortion) due to ASAP2s response kinetics. Cross-measured alongside Archon1 (Archon1 is the paper's primary/newer subject in the AP-width comparison). Distinct from the eLife Fig. 2J (~17.4 ms) and Villette Fig. 2F (~17.3 ms) values — Milosevic used a lower 500 Hz frame rate and different neurons.

photobleach.note: Cross-GEVI photobleaching from the same Fig. S4 46 mW/mm² panel as JEDI-1P (470/24-nm widefield, HEK293T, n=12). Relative-to-ASAP2s trace renormalized to ASAP2s's own first frame → F/F0. ASAP2s drops to F/F0≈0.72 in the first ~3 s (shared rapid-initial-photobleach transient) then PLATEAUS — it does not fall to 50% within the 2-min window, so no half-life (t50) is stored (fractionally very photostable after the transient; the biexp slow component τ2 is poorly constrained). Curve retained for cross-overlay. As with ASAP3, ASAP2s shows less fractional bleaching than the brighter JEDI-1P; JEDI-1P's advantage in this figure is photon budget (absolute brightness).

Cross-GEVI entry from the JEDI-2P origin paper (primary subject JEDI-2P): 1P photobleaching of ASAP2s in HEK293-Kir2.1 cells, widefield 1PM at 15 mW/mm² over 5 min (Fig. S4G, blue curve). ASAP2s has the LARGEST fast-bleach component here — it loses ~half its fluorescence within ~3 s (to ~0.42) then declines slowly to ~0.29. Both the t75% and t50 crossings therefore fall inside this fast transient (t50 ≈ 3 s); the stored t50 honestly reflects that ASAP2s is dominated by rapid initial photobleaching under 1PM (caption: 'larger fast-photobleaching component'), not a digitization artifact. Note this is a higher-intensity, longer recording than the Fig. S4 JEDI-1P-paper entry above (which renormalized a relative-brightness trace and plateaued). Biexponential fit. Companion curves: JEDI-2P, ASAP3.

ASAP2s (green) biexponential τ1=121 s (69%) + τ2=1017 s (31%) per Fig. 1-fig.suppl.3B. t75≈50 s, t50≈134 s. Origin-paper 1P photobleach, Fig. 1—figure supplement 3A: HEK293-Kir2.1 cells, continuous widefield 470–490 nm at 11 mW/mm², emission 525/50 nm, normalized to 1.0 at t=0. The stored curve is generated from the paper's OWN published fit (Fig. 1—figure supplement 3B) rather than pixel-digitized, because ASAP1/ASAP2s/ArcLight overlap almost exactly in panel A; the fit endpoints match the panel-A traces at 5 min. All co-plotted sensors (ASAP1, ASAP2s, ArcLight Q239; ASAP1::EGFP has no page) share this source+sourceFigure so they cross-overlay.

spectrum.name: ASAP2s
spectrum.note: No one-photon excitation/emission spectrum is published for ASAP1/ASAP2s: the eLife 2017 paper reports only two-photon excitation spectra (Fig. 1E) and the St-Pierre 2014 ASAP1 paper reports none. ASAP1's fluorophore is cpsfGFP-OPT, a circularly permuted superfolder GFP, so the FPbase Superfolder GFP excitation/emission curve is used as a fluorophore proxy. ASAP2s (ASAP1 + R415Q) shares this fluorophore. Replaces a curve that was a verbatim copy of the FPbase EGFP spectrum.

custom.note: Re-digitized from the green ASAP2s trace in Fig. 1C (steady-state %ΔF/F vs membrane potential) using scripts/extract_curve.py: HSV green-channel mask sampled at 10-mV steps and normalized to ΔF/F = 0 at -70 mV. Replaces a prior curve that included an off-chart -120 mV point and step voltages that did not match the figure.
